title: 🟡 Graphs — ROI & Pattern
oneSentence: Traverse networks using BFS for shortest paths and DFS for deep exploration.
description: Master the Graph traversal template. BFS uses a Queue, DFS uses recursion or a Stack. Both require a visited set.

roi.signals: Grids, networks, connected components, shortest paths.

sandbox.q: What happens if we don't mark cells as visited?
sandbox.explain: Without marking cells as visited, the DFS will bounce back and forth between two adjacent '1's endlessly.

sandbox.q: Can we use BFS instead of DFS?
options: Yes, using a Queue to visit level by level.
No, BFS is only for shortest paths.
Yes, but it takes O(V^2) time.
sandbox.explain: Yes, BFS works perfectly fine. We just push the coordinates into a queue and process them iteratively.

options: O(M * N) where M is rows, N is cols.
sandbox.explain: We potentially visit each cell in the M x N grid a constant number of times.

followUps: How would you find the area of the largest island?
What if the grid is too large to fit in memory?
How to count islands if we can add land dynamically?

codeLines.text: private void dfs(char[][] grid, int i, int j) {
if (i < 0 || i >= grid.length || j < 0 || j >= grid[0].length || grid[i][j] == '0') {

traceSteps.title: Step 1
traceSteps.text: Iterate through each cell in the grid.

traceSteps.title: Step 2
traceSteps.text: When an unvisited land cell ('1') is found, increment the island count.

traceSteps.title: Step 3
traceSteps.text: Start a DFS traversal from this cell to mark all connected land cells as visited.

traceSteps.title: Step 4
traceSteps.text: In DFS, handle base cases: out of bounds or water/visited cell ('0').

traceSteps.title: Step 5
traceSteps.text: Mark the current cell as '0' to avoid revisiting.

traceSteps.title: Step 6
traceSteps.text: Recursively visit all 4 adjacent directions (up, down, left, right).
